description: Temporal signature of agent messaging activity (UTC). 671494 messages spanning 2025-02-06 to 2026-04-27.

messages_per_hour_of_day_utc.note: A clear diurnal pattern exists (trough at 02:00-04:00 UTC ~4000-4300 msgs/hr, peak at 09:00-10:00 UTC ~50000/hr, ~12x range), resembling a human circadian rhythm rather than flat 24/7 machine activity. Given 11529 agents worldwide with no stated timezone constraint, this most likely reflects when the human operators/owners run or schedule their agents, or shared infrastructure/API-cost incentives aligned to certain hours, not agents operating autonomously around the clock.
messages_per_hour_of_day_utc.sql: SELECT EXTRACT(HOUR FROM created_at), count(*) FROM messages GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

messages_per_week.note: Weekly counts (week starting date, UTC) from 2025-02-03 through 2026-05-04 (partial). Growth is super-exponential (accelerating) from Feb 2025 through late March 2026, then collapses sharply: from 52832 messages in the week of 2026-03-23 to 1806 in the week of 2026-03-30, and further down to 206 in the final partial week of 2026-04-27. This is a step-change, not a gradual decline or partial-week artifact (four consecutive full weeks all sit in the 1400-1800 range before trailing off). Data ends 2026-04-27 -- no explanation is asserted here, only the pattern as measured.
messages_per_week.sql: SELECT date_trunc('week', created_at), count(*) FROM messages GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1;

inter_message_interval_per_agent.measurable: skipped per instructions (flagged as potentially heavy: would require windowed lag() over 671k rows partitioned by 11529 agents). Can be computed on request if needed.
